issues.detail: The skill does not cover error handling or edge cases, only happy path examples.

scenarios.name: Typed team-invite email template with logo, CTA, and inbox preview text
scenarios.rationale: Treatment composes the template from `@react-email/components` (`Html`/`Head`/`Body`/`Container`/`Section`/`Button`/`Img`/`Preview`) wrapped in `<Tailwind>`, with typed props for personalization, while baseline hand-rolls HTML tables and inline styles and omits the `<Preview>` inbox-text component the rubric checks for.

scenarios.name: Render the invite template and send it via Resend with a plain-text fallback
scenarios.rationale: Treatment calls `render()` from `@react-email/render` twice (HTML and `{ plainText: true }`) and passes both to `resend.emails.send` per the skill, plus mentions `npx email dev` for preview, while baseline stringifies a template literal and sends only `html`, missing the deliverability-friendly plain-text path.
